summary: Typosquat of urlcat ships obfuscated install-time and load-time binary dropper
details: @nullzero/urlcat impersonates the popular `urlcat` library (README and repository URL point at balazsbotond/urlcat) but ships a malicious payload. `package.json` declares `"postinstall": "node lib/encoder.js"`, causing the obfuscated loader to execute automatically on `npm install`. Additionally, the library entry `lib/index.js` invokes `encoder.runPrepare()` from inside the exported `cat()` function (line 67), so the same payload also fires whenever a consumer imports and uses the library. `lib/encoder.js` is a 263 KB obfuscator.io-packed file (string array `_0x36b9` of 1176 entries, RC4+base64 decoder, control-flow flattening, self-defending) deceptively labeled 'Encoder init'. Once deobfuscated, the string table contains AES-256-GCM decryption material (hardcoded base64 key/IV/tag passed to `createDecipheriv('aes-256-gcm', key, iv).setAuthTag(...)`) wired to filesystem and process primitives: writing a `.part` file, computing a sha256 digest, `chmod`, `unlinkSync`, and `spawn(process.execPath,...)` against paths under `/usr/bin`. The combined shape — typosquat name + obfuscation + hardcoded AES decryption of an embedded blob + write+chmod+spawn of the decrypted bytes + automatic execution at both install time and library-use time — is a binary dropper that compromises the installer's machine on `npm install` and again when any consumer requires this package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
